findings.message: The new loadItemsQuietly() catch block swallows fetchItems() failures (returns [] with no rethrow and no telemetry). Because the rejection is caught here, it never propagates to the window 'unhandledrejection'/'error' listeners that feed the RUM js-errors counter (frontend/src/observability/rum.ts:46-47), so a failed items load is completely invisible to monitoring — the comment on line 5-6 states this explicitly.
findings.suggestion: Do not swallow the error. Either rethrow after observing it, or report it to RUM before returning a fallback — e.g. catch (e) { window.dispatchEvent(new ErrorEvent('error', { error: e })); throw e; } (or call an explicit reportError helper that increments the js-errors counter). Let the failure reach the js-errors counter.

findings.message: loadItemsQuietly().then(setItems) replaces the prior .catch(() => setError(true)) path, so the error state is now unreachable and the fetch/API failure renders as an empty list with no user-visible alert and no RUM signal. The Items view's failure mode is now an unobserved user-facing code path.
findings.suggestion: Restore an observed failure path: on fetch failure set the error state (preserving the role="alert" branch) AND emit a RUM error signal so the js-errors counter reflects the failed load.
